content.problemStatement:
Alice recently converted all the positive integers from 111 to 2N−12^N - 12N−1 (both inclusive) into binary strings and stored them in an array SSS.  Note that the binary strings **do not have leading zeroes**.

While she was out, Bob **sorted** all the elements of SSS in lexicographical increasing order.

Let SiS_iSi​ denotes the ithi^{th}ith string in the **sorted array**.

Alice defined a function FFF such that F(Si)F(S_i)F(Si​) is equal to the count of 111 in the string SiS_iSi​.

For example, F(101)=2F(101) = 2F(101)=2 and F(1000)=1F(1000) = 1F(1000)=1.

Given a positive integer KKK, find the value of ∑i=1KF(Si)\sum_{i = 1}^K F(S_i)∑i=1K​F(Si​).

String PPP is lexicographically smaller than string QQQ if one of the following satisfies:


PPP is a prefix of QQQ and P≠QP \neq QP=Q.
There exists an index iii such that Pi<QiP_i < Q_iPi​<Qi​ and for all j<ij < ij<i,  Pj=QjP_j=Q_jPj​=Qj​.

Input Format

The first line will contain an integer TTT - number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two integers NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output the value ∑i=1KF(Si)\sum_{i = 1}^K F(S_i)∑i=1K​F(Si​).
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105.
